stem: A student must pass three sequential exams to earn a certification. The probability of passing Exam 1 is 0.85. If Exam 1 is passed, the probability of passing Exam 2 is between 0.70 and 0.80 (depending on how much the student learned from Exam 1 preparation). If Exam 2 is passed, the probability of passing Exam 3 is 0.90. What is the probability the student earns the certification?

ground_truth.answer: P(certification) ranges from 0.536 to 0.612 as P(Exam 2 | Exam 1 passed) varies from 0.70 to 0.80. Point estimate at 0.75: 0.574.

solution_steps: The uncertainty in Exam 2's pass rate creates a 7.6 percentage point range in the certification probability.
common_failure_modes: Adding probabilities instead of multiplying for sequential events
Using a single value for P(E2|E1) without acknowledging the range
Not recognizing that the exams must be passed in sequence (treating them as independent)
